author: yurizon
body:
Sushi is great, but there are many many more great food, that is just unknown to the western world.

For example Tempura, Soba, Okonomiyaki, Sukiyaki, Wagyu etc.

In fact, the japanese cuisine itself is a UNESCO cultural heritage. The only other cuisine with the same status is the french cuisine. And food in Japan is actually surprisingly cheap, even compared to their purchase power.

author: Successful_Eye_267
body: I feel like there is a stigma as a man living alone , in a residential neighborhood or anywhere really  . I worked hard to own a home but feel out of place sometimes . What got me thinking about it , was the recent Idaho college murders that’s been big news lately . Clearly the guy had some mental health problems and was a bad apple all the way around . But when they were describing him, they were saying things like “ lived alone” “lone wolf” “ vacuumed late at night “ as if it was a bad thing a  man lived alone . See I have friends and stuff that stop by here and there but all that guys neighbors said the same thing that he “lived alone” or whatever . Made me think like yikes do my neighbors think I’m gonna do something like that because some of them don’t know me and I live alone .

author: Successful_Eye_267
body: Sounds like the perfect night, and those wax warmers are the greatest! I’ve been on a bourbon Pecan melt scent lately, smells like home to me. I have it running all the time . You’ve just inspired me to have one of those nights tonight! I’m going to have to come up with a dish I would like to try that I never cooked . Cheers to you as well!

author: FlameHawkfish88
body:
Dude did it ever cross your mind that you're the one being rude? You're blaming everyone else because we can't fix how you feel on a subreddit, but you also seem to be actively seeking out conflict and dumping your negativity on people.

This is casual conversation for fun. You honestly should show a bit more gratitude that people are taking the time to answer you instead of being a grouch about it.

author: Actual-Necessary5051
body:
First take the time to actually read ALL of my replies and then come tell me I showed no gratitude. Except you won’t because that’s not true. I resent you making that conclusion without you making your due diligence.

Second of all, I never said I wasn’t rude.

Third of all, asking for help and demanding it are two completely different things. I make no demands in my post. As for my response, if one goes out of their way to answer a post such as mine the way the first commentator did on this thread, you better believe it I’ll call them out on it. Answering to my post with “well, here is what’s wrong/negative about you. When the same happens to me, I don’t do that and you shouldn’t either. Also just look at the positive, ok? Thanks bye!” I mean, honestly, what the fuck is helpful about that?

No one owes me any help but if they’re gonna attempt it then I’m gonna expect an actual real attempt and I believe belittling me by simplifying things the way the first person did is not cool for the type of post I made so I made that clear. I do not take that back nor do I see that as in any way demanding if help. If you do, then you’re welcome to feel that way. 🤷‍♀️

author: FlameHawkfish88
body:
No one's belittling you, you're just being called out on your own behaviour in the thread, which you seem very comfortable about doing to other people but don't seem to like when the shoe is on the other foot.

I did read your other replies, which is why I commented. If it was on one thread I wouldn't have bothered. 

Saying ok thanks with no further comment or engagement isn't the same as gratitude, this is casual conversation and  it's just shutting down certain threads while arguing on other threads because they said something that is challenging. You come across as very defensive

author: Actual-Necessary5051
body:
There is no shoe on any foot. No one is doing to me whatever I’m doing to them. That comment makes no sense.

And I disagree. I do think the first commentator belittled me when she simplified things on her comment to me. You are welcome to disagree like you already do. 🤷‍♀️ 

No, I don’t believe you read all of my replies. I don’t believe that for a second. Not only that but also… you do know you aren’t the authority on what is gratitude and how it’s supposed to be offered, right? I resent you making your own ideas on it and trying to shame me for not having complied with them.

And yeah, OF COURSE a person who posted what I did would act defensively. I mean… tks captain obvious! Lol So…?

Also, thanks for being another example of sucky humans. Came in here to accuse and complain while offering no help whatsoever but I’m the problem here. Alright then! 🙄😂

author: FlameHawkfish88
body:
Mhmm ok then. I see my error you're perfect and we're all just idiots out to deliberately misunderstand you.

I did post my suggestions in a separate thread to this btw.

Look at me making up my own definition of gratitude based on them checks notes.. actually definition of gratitude 

"the quality of being thankful; readiness to show appreciation for and to return kindness."

author: Actual-Necessary5051
body:
Oh, yes, definitely I’m so perfect! I’m here asking for help on something major that idk how to do when it comes so easily to what seems to be everyone else but yeah no, I totally think I’m perfect! 🙄🤦‍♀️ Holy cow! Lol

Oh I don’t look at usernames so if you posted on a different reply I likely already replied to it, just didn’t know it was you.

And yeah, hun I readily showed gratitude by saying thank you. If it’s not enough to you that’s your problem, don’t try and make it mine.

Ooohh waitasecond! Are you the one who edited their comment after I had replied to it saying you’re Aussie? Yeah, well, I said alright, thanks because your original comment didn’t have much to offer, in my opinion. It basically just said “hey, give people the benefit of the doubt, we are all tying to live our lives best we can” so I simply said, “alright, thanks.” The fact you’re basing my reply to you as not enough gratitude shows to me you have at least a hint of narcissism. I mean, who the hell hears a thank you and turns around and says, nope, you’re not being grateful enough. If thank you is not enough to you then how about a fuck off? Is that to your satisfaction?

author: Icy-Town-5355
body:
WAIT. I do this for a living... I onboard new hires  for a living, meaning I run their background checks, process their paperwork, and their physicals. I sometimes see people give notice as soon as a recruiter says they will extend an offer..... UNLESS you can afford to not work for a couple of extra weeks, wait until you have a s9lid offer IN WRITING. NOT just an offer letter, but a solid, "post background check" offer. If the hiring company does a full background check,  a lot of things can go sideways,  such as a delay in a criminal background check.... some states (hello GA!) are absolutely terrible in cleaning a criminal check. Also, past employers are often VERY slow in verifying employment, and some never do it at all. You may have to produce W2s or, if you're a contractor, 1099s to prove you worked for a company. If you have a physical,  your primary care physician may have to provide documentation to clear you for employment.  Some employers don't have such rigorous screening, but check with the hiring company what their process is before you give notice.  The average onboarding process at my company is 3 weeks. I advise my hires to wait until they have been fully cleared and onboarded for hire and have a solid offer letter IN HAND.

BEST WISHES TO YOU!!

author: LesGetLunch
body:
You’re so thoughtful. I appreciate you so much! I have been talking with this doctor since before I even applied. She has been reassuring me for 2 months that the job is mine and that I need not worry. It has taken a long time (2 months) because of holidays and other situation at new employment with some audit or something of the sorts. Every indication says the job is mine besides the actual letter which should HOPEFULY already (and supposedly has been written and sent but due to them closing the job so nobody else would apply) be on its way just waiting for some minor detail. Even so I have not done it yet and plan to go speak with my new boss (the job is same institution but different department) to ensure that I can put in my 2 weeks and see if it’s ok with her that I start in February as I’d like to take a small 2 week break in between this job and the next. Thankfully she has seen me hard at work and even saw me coming in during Christmas closer and during this last week I took a vacation and still “had” to come in. (No overtime pay or nothing)

My family has advised me to do as you say but right now I’d really like to take that vacation, and if that means I lose the job and have to go find a different one then I’ll take this as a well deserved break and learn and never do this again. I just really want to go see my friend and hopefully later that week A GIRL 😂

author: smelling_the_roses
body:
I feel for you.

Relationships are hard, but it can be good to see how people really “are” when things aren’t perfect and rosy. Helps us know how to navigate future interactions. 

Similarly, tonight, I had a bad disagreement with a cousin I used to respect. Think our friendship is beyond repair.

Hope you two are able to get past it and move forward.

author: Fluffy_Bend_5776
body: I really hope so! I still have nothing against them and I will be glad to talk to this friend if they are willing to talk and if both of us can take responsibilities for what we did. If that doesn't happen that's fine too, afterall we are two different individuals and we have our own choices. From my personal experience, once you allow one person to step on your boundaries and forget about yourself, it won't just be one person or one instance. that's why I always value communication.  Maybe my friend has different values and it's fine. We will still grow as individuals but maybe not together.

author: smelling_the_roses
body:
Boundaries are hugely important. I learned that much later than I should have. 

You sound wise, especially that last line. Think I really needed to hear that tonight.

Cheers to happier days ahead :)

author: NerdDwarf
body:
Children are a responsibility

Don't treat them like a gift to others. They're not

They are living breathing things that need to be cared for and looked after. 

They will be rambunctious. They will be loud. They will be annoying. They will cause sleep deprivation. They will make life difficult at every step. They will cost you lots of money. This is normal. This should be met with gentle, loving care.

Never blame them for being expensive. Never blame them for disturbing you.

You brought them into this world. They did not ask for this. They do not know how the world functions. Not just politics or interpersonal relationships. They won't know that cars move when they're not looking at them.

---The child of a "woman" who believed having children made her life better and then spent 19 years abusing them for not innately acting the way she had dreamed of, while simultaneously blaming them for every single mistake they ever made

author: Trick_Olive_7998
body: I def will, i still feel like a child lol im not having kids until at least a few years later when i actually feel grown

author: Trick_Olive_7998
body: I will😊 i wont have kids until im at least 23 but i think abt these kind of things sometimes haha

author: Trick_Olive_7998
body: That’s really nice to hear! Grandmas really are a blessing and im sure your daughter is lucky to have her🥺 i can only imagine how nice it feels seeing their daughter-grandma relationship, i hope it lasts a long time❤️

author: Trick_Olive_7998
body: No offense because you’re right about everything you said but i did not need this, i know all of this stuff and just because i mentioned ONE con of having kids doesnt mean that’s the sole reason i’ll be having them. I didnt know i’ll need to clarity this but i posted this because i was just casually thinking of the future and stuff and this is one of the many things that came to my mind, it’s not because my main goal in life is to just gift kids to people. I know kids arent just some dolls you have for fun but they require real care and love, i know the hardships of having kids and how life works. I love kids myself and i want to have them in the future because that’s just how i feel and hopefully i’ll be a great mom when the time comes, it’s just that while thinking of this I imagined how happy my mom will be when she becomes a grandma so i wanted to share that. Believe me i wont just carelessly have kids out of selfishness, i know they deserve loving parents who will take care of them mentally, physically and financially while acknowledging that they’re humans and not accessories
